steps.title: Identify the specific demand in the underlined claim
steps.content: The claim states “strong support for building more HSR lines in the United States.” The phrase “building more HSR lines” specifies exactly what the survey response must advocate for.

steps.title: Match the advocacy to the choices
steps.content: Choice C says “The United States needs to build more high-speed rail lines,” which directly endorses the expansion the claim describes. Choices A and D prefer alternatives or oppose further investment, while B merely reports having used HSR elsewhere without expressing any position on U.S. expansion. That matches choice C — C is correct.
